𝗚𝗢𝗢𝗚𝗟𝗘-𝗠𝗔𝗣𝗦-𝗦𝗖𝗥𝗔𝗣𝗣𝗘𝗥 | Automated extraction of business information from Google Maps for analysis and integration into other systems.  ✨ Notable!

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Automated extraction of business information from Google Maps for analysis and integration into other systems.
📅 Created: Oct 2023 • Updated: May 2026

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Scrapes business data from Google Maps (name, address, website, phone, reviews, hours, etc.)
✅ Extracts review counts and average ratings
✅ Detects business service types (in-store shopping, pickup, delivery)
✅ Extracts introductory business information
✅ Cleanses and organizes scraped data
✅ Exports cleaned data to a CSV file


✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 886 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 186 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

This Python repository uses Playwright to scrape business information from Google Maps, extracting details like name, address, website, reviews, and operating hours. It cleans the data and exports it to a CSV file for further analysis.

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/zohaibbashir/Google-Maps-Scrapper

𝗦𝗦𝗛-𝗔𝗜-𝗖𝗛𝗔𝗧 | To provide a seamless and accessible way for users to chat with AI models directly from their terminal via SSH, without needing a graphical interface.  🔥 Super Hot!

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a seamless and accessible way for users to chat with AI models directly from their terminal via SSH, without needing a graphical interface.
📅 Created: Jun 2025 • Updated: May 2026

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Chat with AI over SSH
✅ Supported on macOS (iTerm2, Ghostty)
✅ Backend using Node.js and SSH2
✅ UI Framework using React and Ink
✅ Database support for PostgreSQL and Redis
✅ Docker deployment option
✅ Configurable AI models and system prompts
✅ Rate limiting and access control (blacklist/whitelist)


◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 826 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 57 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 4 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

SSH AI Chat allows users to interact with AI models directly through an SSH client. It provides a command-line interface experience for chatting with AI, supporting various terminals and offering deployment options via Docker.

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗟𝗦𝗩 | A fast, keyboard-centric, and highly configurable file manager for the terminal, enhancing productivity for developers and power users.  🔥 Super Hot!

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: A fast, keyboard-centric, and highly configurable file manager for the terminal, enhancing productivity for developers and power users.
📅 Created: Sep 2025 • Updated: May 2026

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Three-pane navigation (parent/current/preview)
✅ Keyboard-driven UX with multi-key sequences and which-key overlay
✅ Lua configuration for themes, keymaps, actions, and previewer
✅ External command integration for previews
✅ File operations (add/rename/delete, copy/move/paste)
✅ Marks for saving and jumping to directories
✅ Display modes (hidden files, size/date formatting)
✅ Command palette with suggestions and Tab-completion


❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

⭐⭐⭐ 111 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 3 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

lsv is a terminal-based file browser written in Rust, offering a three-pane interface for navigating parent, current, and preview directories. It's highly configurable via Lua and designed for keyboard-driven interaction with features like multi-key sequences and a which-key overlay.

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗘𝗧𝗘𝗥𝗡𝗔𝗟𝗩𝗢𝗪𝗦 | Creating a personalized, self-hosted wedding website to share event information.  🔥 Super Hot!

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Creating a personalized, self-hosted wedding website to share event information.
📅 Created: Aug 2025 • Updated: May 2026

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Customizable, self-hosted wedding website template
✅ Config-driven content via YAML or JSON (names, date, location, story, schedule, venues, registry, FAQs)
✅ Dynamic background slideshow with auto-refreshing images
✅ Clean, modern single-page design
✅ Lightweight Node.js + Express server
✅ Easy deployment with Docker/Compose


❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

🍴 3 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Eternal Vows is a lightweight, self-hosted wedding website template. It allows users to easily configure and display all essential wedding details, such as names, dates, venues, stories, schedules, and registry links, through a simple YAML or JSON file. The template features a clean, modern design with a dynamic background slideshow powered by a Node.js server.

𝗚𝗨𝗔𝗥𝗗𝗗𝗢𝗚 | To scan and identify malicious software packages in various development ecosystems, enhancing software supply chain security.  🔥 Super Hot!

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To scan and identify malicious software packages in various development ecosystems, enhancing software supply chain security.
📅 Created: Jun 2022 • Updated: May 2026

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Identifies malicious PyPI and npm packages
✅ Supports Go modules, RubyGems, GitHub actions, and VSCode extensions
✅ Scans local or remote packages
✅ Utilizes Semgrep rules for source code analysis
✅ Analyzes package metadata
✅ Provides CLI for scanning and verification
✅ Offers JSON and SARIF output formats
✅ Integrates with Datadog Agent and Cloud SIEM


⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 96 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 32 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

GuardDog is a command-line interface (CLI) tool designed to identify malicious packages across various ecosystems, including PyPI, npm, Go, RubyGems, GitHub Actions, and VSCode extensions. It achieves this by analyzing package source code using Semgrep rules and examining package metadata to detect potential threats.

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀

🔍 32 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦◦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

GuardDog is a command-line interface (CLI) tool designed to identify malicious packages across various ecosystems including PyPI, npm, Go, RubyGems, GitHub Actions, and VSCode extensions. It employs heuristics and Semgrep rules to analyze both package source code and metadata for potential threats.
